headline: Oil market gripped by record volatility and speculation since start of Middle East war
description: Brent crude and diesel prices have surged since the start of the US-Israeli military intervention in Iran. Behind the scenes, major trading firms and oil giants have monitored every move of oil tankers and watched closely for any drone attack.

headline: War in Iran: At the G7, Canada and its partners want clarification from Washington
United States and Israel are facing the same Iranian foe but are fighting different wars
Baltic states fear Ukraine war could spill over onto their territory
description: Two Ukrainian drones launched against Russia accidentally landed in Estonia and Latvia. This incident comes as the scenario of a fictional pro-Russian republic, orchestrated by Moscow to justify a military intervention, spreads in the Baltic states.

headline: French spirits giant Pernod Ricard in merger talks with Jack Daniel's owner
description: The discussions come as the alcohol sector faces a difficult global environment, with consumption falling among younger drinkers and new tariffs – particularly in the United States – hurting major producers. Pernod Ricard has been especially affected by US duties.

headline: Napoleon's long-lost hat to go on display
description: Made by Poupard, Napoleon's official hatmaker, the headpiece displays all the hallmark features of an authentic imperial bicorne, including its distinctive proportions, a small tricolor cockade and a silk-taffeta lining. It will be exhibited at the Chateau de Chantilly, north of Paris, later in the year.

headline: They were adopted and raised middle-class. France saw them as Black and Arab
description: Youssef Dridi and Eyob Bressy, both adopted and raised by families with significant cultural capital in the Paris region, share how, from adolescence, they experienced discrimination due to their skin color.

headline: Trump pushes back deadline for strikes on Iran's energy assets
Israeli army calls for more troops on Lebanese front, opposition warns shortage could trigger 'security disaster'
US currency to bear Trump's signature in first for sitting president
Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
Islamic scholar Ramadan sentenced to 18 years in prison for raping three women in 'campaign of psychological destruction'
Night train service resumes between Paris and Berlin
Sarkozy minister's defense unsettled by memo in Libyan campaign funding appeal
IOC brings back gender testing for 2028 Summer Olympics
Thierry Breton: 'A golden rule limiting the public deficit to 1% of GDP must be enshrined in the French Constitution by 2032'
Why the Greens lost several major cities in France's municipal elections
Chilean man sentenced to life in prison in France for murdering ex-girlfriend
EU parliament votes through measures allowing deportation of migrants to 'return hubs'
Regular exchanges between Hungarian and Russian foreign ministers spark scandal in Brussels
description: According to several media outlets, Péter Szijjarto was providing real-time updates to Sergey Lavrov on the content of discussions within the European Union's Foreign Affairs Council.

headline: Zinédine Zidane closes in on leading Les Bleus
description: Less than three months before the 2026 World Cup, the former French maestro is the clear frontrunner to succeed France's football squad head coach Didier Deschamps, who will step down at the end of the competition.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

headline: Trump faces the trap of escalation in Iran
Pierre Buhler, former diplomat: We are witnessing 'a worrying crack in the European edifice'
France's traditional parties dream of a return to the classic left-right contest
